# Metrics & KPIs — AI-Powered Banking & Financial Assistant

---

## 1. Overview

This document defines the key metrics used to evaluate the performance, usability, and business impact of the AI-powered banking assistant. The metrics are divided into three categories:

* Model Metrics (AI performance)
* Product Metrics (user behavior)
* Business Metrics (organizational impact)

---

## 2. Model Metrics (AI Performance)

### 2.1 Response Accuracy

* Definition: Percentage of correct and relevant responses generated by the model
* Target: > 85%
* Importance: Ensures reliability of financial insights

---

### 2.2 Hallucination Rate

* Definition: Percentage of responses containing incorrect or fabricated information
* Target: < 10%
* Importance: Critical in financial domain to maintain trust

---

### 2.3 Response Latency

* Definition: Time taken by the model to generate a response
* Target: < 3 seconds (ideal), < 5 seconds (acceptable)
* Importance: Impacts user experience

---

### 2.4 Relevance Score

* Definition: How well the response matches user intent (measured via feedback or evaluation models)
* Target: High (qualitative + quantitative)
* Importance: Ensures contextual understanding

---

### 2.5 Fallback Rate

* Definition: Percentage of times the model responds with "I don’t know" or fallback message
* Target: Balanced (not too high, not too low)
* Importance: Indicates confidence calibration

---

## 3. Product Metrics (User Behavior)

### 3.1 Daily Active Users (DAU)

* Definition: Number of unique users interacting daily
* Importance: Measures adoption

---

### 3.2 Monthly Active Users (MAU)

* Definition: Number of unique users per month
* Importance: Measures growth

---

### 3.3 Session Duration

* Definition: Average time a user spends per session
* Importance: Indicates engagement

---

### 3.4 Queries per Session

* Definition: Number of interactions per session
* Importance: Reflects usefulness of the system

---

### 3.5 User Retention Rate

* Definition: Percentage of users returning to the platform
* Importance: Measures long-term value

---

### 3.6 User Satisfaction Score

* Definition: Feedback rating (e.g., thumbs up/down or rating system)
* Target: > 4/5
* Importance: Direct indicator of product quality

---

## 4. Business Metrics (Impact)

### 4.1 Reduction in Customer Support Load

* Definition: Decrease in support tickets due to AI automation
* Importance: Cost saving for organization

---

### 4.2 Cost per Query

* Definition: Infrastructure cost per user query
* Importance: Helps optimize scalability

---

### 4.3 Conversion Rate (Loan / Product Adoption)

* Definition: Percentage of users taking action (e.g., applying for loan)
* Importance: Direct revenue impact

---

### 4.4 Customer Engagement Rate

* Definition: Frequency of interactions per user
* Importance: Measures stickiness of product

---

### 4.5 Productivity Improvement

* Definition: Time saved for users (qualitative/quantitative)
* Importance: Key selling point for AI product

---

## 5. AI-Specific Monitoring Metrics

### 5.1 Drift Detection

* Detect changes in input/query patterns over time
* Helps maintain model performance

---

### 5.2 Prompt Effectiveness

* Measure which prompts lead to best outputs
* Optimize prompt engineering

---

### 5.3 Error Rate

* Track failed or incorrect responses
* Used for continuous improvement

---

## 6. Dashboard & Monitoring Tools

* Power BI / Tableau (for visualization)
* Prometheus + Grafana (for system monitoring)
* Custom logging via backend

---

## 7. Success Criteria (Summary)

The system is considered successful if:

* High response accuracy (>85%)
* Low latency (<5 sec)
* Increasing DAU/MAU
* High user satisfaction (>4/5)
* Reduction in support queries

---

## 8. Continuous Improvement Strategy

* Collect user feedback
* Retrain model with new data
* Monitor KPIs regularly
* Optimize API and infrastructure

---

## 9. Summary

These metrics ensure:

* AI system is reliable
* Product is engaging
* Business value is measurable

They enable data-driven decision-making for scaling the product.
